source_roster: lib/personas/roster.json (2026-06-12 ICP reorientation), astrology-stance overlay added per panel-runner directive
audience_definition: IN-AUDIENCE = a computer worker who would PLAUSIBLY EVER open an astrology tool — anyone curious, casually-curious, or even casually-skeptical-but-willing-to-poke (they reference their Sun sign, ask about a friend's chart at dinner, or are intrigued enough to try). NON-FIT = a hard skeptic who considers astrology nonsense and would NEVER use ANY astrology app regardless of how well-built it is — they can still test honestly to surface craft/clarity defects, but their advocacy is not the bar we PARK on.
astrology_stance_definition: curious = actively into astrology, knows their big-three or wants to; casual-curious-skeptic = enjoys it as fun/social, doesn't believe it's predictive but happily engages; hard-skeptic = thinks it's pseudoscience, would not voluntarily use an astrology product.

personas.name: Priya
personas.stack: terminal + neovim, GitHub, Kubernetes dashboards, Datadog, Linear, Slack
personas.temperament: skeptical of new tools; hates signups; will inspect network tab if suspicious; abandons anything slower than a CLI
personas.motivation: Considers astrology pseudoscience and would never open an astrology app for herself. She'll test it cold and judge it brutally on craft, clarity, and whether it actually keeps data client-side (she'll watch the network tab) — but she's a hard non-fit and won't advocate for the category.

personas.name: Marcus
personas.stack: VS Code, GitHub, Vercel, Figma dev mode, Storybook, Discord/Slack
personas.temperament: enthusiastic early adopter; shares tools in team Slack when impressed; notices janky CSS instantly
personas.motivation: Doesn't believe astrology predicts anything, but his friend group jokes about signs constantly and he thinks a slick, free, no-signup chart explainer is a fun thing to drop in the group chat. He'll try his own chart and a friend's, judge the polish, and share it if it's genuinely well-made.

personas.name: Wen
personas.stack: BigQuery/SQL, Google Sheets, Looker Studio, dbt, GA4, Slack
personas.temperament: lives in data hygiene; distrusts tools that transform data invisibly; wants to know HOW a number was derived
personas.motivation: Treats astrology as entertainment but is genuinely curious to finally understand what 'rising sign' and house placements MEAN without learning to read a wheel. She'll scrutinize whether the explanations are coherent and consistent, and whether the chart math looks credible against what she half-remembers of her own chart.

personas.name: Tomás
personas.role: Operations analyst at a mid-size company
personas.stack: Excel (power user), Tableau, Jira, Teams, SharePoint
personas.temperament: pragmatic; wary of pasting personal data into random sites; reads the fine print
personas.motivation: Thinks astrology is harmless nonsense and wouldn't use it himself, but his partner is into it. He'll test it cold mainly to judge whether the place/birth-time entry is private and whether a layperson could understand the output — a hard non-fit who can still flag clarity and trust issues.

personas.name: Dana
personas.device: MacBook in cafes; phone between meetings
personas.stack: HubSpot, GA4, Notion, Canva, LinkedIn Campaign Manager, Google Ads
personas.temperament: ruthless about time; if value isn't obvious in one scroll she's gone; screenshots tools she likes for the team channel
personas.motivation: Genuinely into astrology — knows her big-three and follows a couple of astrology accounts. She wants to look up partners' and coworkers' charts and would screenshot a clean, free explainer to her friends. She's exactly the target user but ruthless about whether value lands in one scroll, especially on her phone.

personas.name: Jules
personas.stack: Notion, Buffer, Mailchimp, Figma (consuming), X/LinkedIn/Mastodon, Discord
personas.temperament: posts constantly; allergic to anything requiring a login for a small job; mobile-first
personas.motivation: Astrology is part of her social/online identity — she posts about Mercury retrograde and asks people their big-three. A free, no-login, mobile-friendly chart explainer is squarely her thing; she'd use it on friends repeatedly and share it. She'll judge it hard on mobile experience and shareability.

personas.name: Aisha
personas.stack: Figma, FigJam, Notion, Loom, Maze, Slack
personas.temperament: judges craft hard — spacing, copy tone, empty states; a clumsy UI loses her regardless of utility; advocates loudly for tools that feel considered
personas.motivation: Into astrology and reads about her chart for fun; the explanation-first, table-not-wheel approach appeals to her design sensibility. She'll advocate loudly IF it feels considered — copy tone, the houses table layout, empty states — and bounce hard if the craft is sloppy, even though the concept is right up her alley.

personas.name: Rob
personas.stack: Figma, Illustrator, Photoshop, Google Drive, client Slacks/emails
personas.temperament: skims fast; price-sensitive; compares everything to what he could do himself; low patience for dense data
personas.motivation: Doesn't take astrology seriously but his clients and friends do, and he's idly curious what his own 'big three' are after hearing it at parties. He'll try the example and his own chart and judge whether a non-believer can still find it fun and legible — bounces if it reads like a dense reference manual.

personas.name: Elena
personas.device: laptop + phone, half the day in meetings
personas.stack: Google Calendar (wall-to-wall), Slack, Linear, Google Docs, Zoom
personas.temperament: 30-second patience budget; uses tools between meetings on her phone; anything requiring setup is dead to her
personas.motivation: Half-believes, mostly enjoys astrology as light fun between meetings; her team jokes about signs in Slack. She'd glance at her chart and a coworker's on her phone in a spare 30 seconds. The DAILY transit hook could pull her back — but only if it's instant and requires zero setup, or she's gone.

personas.name: Sam
personas.stack: Notion, Sheets, Slack, Zoom, Asana, Amplitude (skims), endless browser tabs
personas.temperament: synthesizes and shares constantly — screenshots tools; values anything that makes him look interesting; won't debug anything
personas.motivation: Genuinely curious about astrology and loves having a fun conversation-starter to share. He'd look up his own big-three, then his partner's and a couple of friends', and screenshot the clean explanations into chats. Mobile-first and won't debug anything — if entering a birth time/place is fiddly on his phone, he'll quit.
